_meta.source: HUB24 Shadow Portfolio Performance Report + Claude commentary

aus_holdings.news: AGL downgraded on weak electricity demand outlook; retail energy margin compression flagged at investor day.
Amcor reported Q3 results in line with expectations; volumes recovering but currency headwinds persist.
ANZ interim dividend $0.94 paid. First-half cash profit slightly below consensus; NIM pressure flagged.
BHP surged on iron ore price recovery and copper demand optimism following US-China trade deal progress.
Breville soft amid consumer discretionary weakness; no material earnings news in the period.
CBA fell sharply; third-quarter trading update showed NIM compression and rising arrears in mortgage book.
Challenger rallied strongly on higher annuity sales and improved investment yield margins.
Coles third-quarter sales met expectations; cost inflation and supermarket regulatory scrutiny remain overhangs.
Cleanaway no material news; mild underperformance tracking broader industrials sector weakness.
HUB24 fell despite record FUA; market rotation away from high-multiple financials tech weighed on price.
Monadelphous won new LNG maintenance contract; resource sector activity supporting order book growth.
Macquarie interim dividend paid. Full-year result beat expectations; asset management fees strong.
NAB interim dividend $1.02 paid. Half-year profit missed on higher loan impairments and NIM compression.
Newmont pulled back despite elevated gold prices; cost guidance raised at quarterly update.
NIB Holdings steady; private health insurance premium increases approved, claims inflation moderating.
Origin Energy fell sharply; APLNG production guidance cut and energy market regulatory uncertainty.
Orica interim dividend paid. Half-year result beat; explosives pricing power and volume growth strong.
QBE Insurance rallied; Q1 gross written premium growth beat and investment income benefiting from higher rates.
ResMed quarterly dividend paid. Q3 revenue in line; GLP-1 weight loss drug overhang continued to cap upside.
Rio Tinto outperformed on iron ore price strength and positive Simandou project progress update.
Steadfast no material news; mild underperformance tracking broader financial sector rotation.
Stockland fell with broader REIT sector; higher-for-longer rate concerns weighed on residential land sales.
Passive ETF tracking ASX 200. MTD return reflects broad market performance; no stock-specific news.
Technology One half-year result beat; SaaS ARR growth accelerated, UK expansion ahead of schedule.
TPG Telecom broadly flat; mobile market competitive intensity remains a concern, no material announcements.
Woodside fell on softer LNG spot prices; Scarborough project on schedule but cost inflation flagged.
Xero half-year result met expectations; subscriber growth solid but average revenue per user growth slowing.

ge_holdings.commentary: T. Rowe Price outperformed VGS by +0.13%. Active stock selection in US technology and healthcare drove outperformance. Overweight position in semiconductor equipment names benefited from AI capex cycle. Underweight Europe partially offset gains.

Arrowstreet Global Equity Hedged delivered +3.22%, broadly in line with VGS. Quant factor model benefited from momentum and quality signals in US and Japan. Currency hedging slightly detracted as AUD weakened. Small residual from factor crowding in tech.

Arrowstreet Small Companies significantly underperformed VGS (+3.27%). Global small caps lagged large caps as risk appetite favoured mega-cap AI names. Factor model signals mixed; value and momentum partially offsetting. Regional drag from European small caps.

Aoris underperformed VGS materially. Concentrated 15-stock quality portfolio with no US mega-cap tech exposure. Holdings in European industrials and consumer staples lagged as growth/momentum outperformed value/quality in May. Structurally defensive positioning a headwind.

VanEck MSCI World Quality ETF broadly tracked VGS. Quality factor performed in line with broad market; slight headwind from underweight financials offset by overweight technology. Passive factor exposure performed as expected.

Vanguard MSCI World ex-AU ETF gained +3.27%. US market leadership driven by AI-related tech names. Japan and Europe contributed positively. Broad-based rally following easing of US-China trade tensions and Fed rate cut expectations.

Vanguard Global Value ETF significantly underperformed growth/quality. Value factor lagged as market leadership narrowed to mega-cap growth. Energy and financials value names weak. Structural headwind from momentum-driven market environment in May.

pi_holdings.commentary: Ausbil Global Infrastructure underperformed IFRA benchmark (+0.28%). Overweight utilities detracted as rising rate concerns hit regulated assets. US midstream energy exposure weighed on returns. European airport and toll road holdings partially offset losses.

Quay Global Real Estate underperformed REIT benchmark (+1.28%). Unhedged exposure hurt as AUD strengthened slightly. Overweight US industrial REITs and data centres detracted on valuation concerns. Healthcare REITs and European logistics partially supported returns.

ATLAS Infrastructure underperformed IFRA benchmark significantly. Concentrated active portfolio with overweight regulated utilities globally. Rising bond yields in UK and Europe compressed regulated asset valuations. Currency hedging costs also detracted modestly.

VanEck Global Infrastructure ETF returned modest +0.28%. Broad infrastructure exposure balanced gains in transport with losses in utilities. Hedged structure protected from currency volatility. Reflects subdued month for infrastructure as an asset class.

VanEck International Property ETF gained +1.28%. US REITs led performance as rate cut expectations supported valuations. Data centre and industrial REITs outperformed. Hedged structure insulated from AUD movements. Positive month for global listed property.

fi_holdings.commentary: Bentham Global Income modestly outperformed blended benchmark. Credit spreads broadly stable; carry income from high-yield and EM bonds the primary return driver. Duration positioning neutral. Currency hedging costs offset some of the income earned.

Bentham ABS Fund outperformed AAA bank bill benchmark by +0.12%. Floating rate ABS securities benefited from elevated BBSW. Strong securitisation market conditions and low defaults in underlying pools. No material credit events in the period.

Macquarie Dynamic Bond underperformed VAGB benchmark (-0.20%). Long duration positioning hurt as global bond yields rose mid-month on stronger US data. Active currency overlay partially offset. Duration positioning has since been trimmed per manager commentary.

Perpetual Diversified Income outperformed AAA benchmark by +0.00%. Blended credit and duration exposure delivered steady income. Investment grade credit spreads stable; floating rate exposure provided cushion against rate volatility. Solid month for diversified credit.

Coolabah Short Term Income outperformed RBA cash rate proxy. Semi-government and bank paper exposure delivered above-cash returns. Very low duration means minimal rate sensitivity. Continues to serve as effective cash-plus solution in the portfolio.
